era_context: The Early Bronze Age III in the Levant and Mesopotamia; the Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin; Egypt's Old Kingdom in decline; the Indus Valley Civilization in its mature phase; early Bronze Age cultures across the Aegean and Anatolia.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia beyond the Indus and early Chinese cultures
Most of Mesopotamia and Egypt (no year-specific documentation)
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The year 2402 BCE is not distinguished in any known historical record. No specific events are documented for this year. The historical record for this period does not provide year-by-year documentation; instead, we have general knowledge of the broader era (Akkadian Empire, Indus Valley Civilization, Egyptian Old Kingdom decline) but no specific events assignable to this particular year. The absence of documentation reflects both the limitations of ancient record-keeping and the loss of records over millennia.
